signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + BoJ Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.53, breaking above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. Trump's threat of hard strikes on Iran sparks risk-off sentiment, but the 287bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.60% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid. JPY COT LF net short -105,136 (CFTC, week ending Jun 02) indicates crowded carry trades, setting up short-squeeze risk if BoJ intervenes or shifts hawkish at the next meeting. The options market shows moderate put skew (USD/JPY 25d RR -1.19), suggesting some hedging for JPY strength despite the spot move. Watch for BoJ comments or direct intervention above 160.50.

signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + CESI + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6998. The G10 scorecard shows AUD as the weakest G10 currency this week (avg -1.288% vs 4 pairs) while USD is the strongest (avg +0.957% vs 7 pairs), creating a 2.245% leader-to-laggard spread. This bilateral theme is reinforced by AUD's moderate negative CESI (net -9 misses) indicating disappointing data, and a 75bp AUD carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.60%) that is failing to support the pair. Options market shows moderate put skew (AUD/USD 25d RR -1.15), aligning with the bearish sentiment. Watch for RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun for any shift in stance.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy + COT vs OIS Divergence + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1535. The ECB policy announcement is the main event today, with the market bias for a hike. However, EUR COT LF net short -22,320 (CFTC, week ending Jun 02) indicates speculative positioning is fading this hawkish bias. This COT vs OIS divergence creates short-squeeze risk if the ECB delivers a hawkish surprise. Options market shows elevated vol premium (EUR/USD IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.3%, VRP +2.8%) and a mild put skew (25d RR -0.63), suggesting hedging demand for EUR downside despite the potential for a hawkish ECB. A clear hawkish signal from the ECB is likely to trigger a rapid unwind of spec shorts.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3361. The options market is pricing a significant volatility premium, with IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.4%, resulting in a VRP of +4.6%. This indicates strong hedging demand above realized volatility. The 25d RR at -0.81 shows a moderate put skew, signaling options traders are paying for GBP weakness protection. This suggests underlying caution despite GBP COT LF net long +27,022 (CFTC, week ending Jun 02). The elevated VRP and put skew imply potential for downside volatility or a sustained move lower. Watch for a break below 1.3350 to confirm bearish options sentiment.
